# notifications — ARCHITECTURE.md
# Last Updated: 2026-06-02
# Status: CURRENT SOURCE OF TRUTH — generated by ARCHITECT 2026-06-02

## Feature Overview

The `notifications` feature manages the full lifecycle of in-app notification delivery
for VCSM actors: publishing events via the `@notifications` engine, reading and rendering
the inbox with bidirectional block filtering, tracking unread badge counts, and dispatching
typed notification views per event kind (booking, comment, follow, mention, reaction,
review, team). It also owns the `My Appointments` panel, which surfaces booking state
to the authenticated citizen via the `@booking` engine.

**Source Path:** apps/VCSM/src/features/notifications/
**Engine Path:** engines/notifications/ (consumed via `@notifications` alias)
**Secondary Engines:** engines/hydration/ (via `@hydration` alias), engines/booking/ (via `@booking` alias in screen hook)

---

## Layer Presence

| Layer | Present | Path |

| Controllers | YES | `apps/VCSM/src/features/notifications/inbox/controller/` |

| Models | YES | `apps/VCSM/src/features/notifications/inbox/model/notification.model.js` + `apps/VCSM/src/features/notifications/runtime/notificationRuntime.model.js` |
| Hooks | YES | `apps/VCSM/src/features/notifications/inbox/hooks/` + `apps/VCSM/src/features/notifications/screen/hooks/` |
| Screens | YES | `apps/VCSM/src/features/notifications/screen/` |
| Components | YES | `apps/VCSM/src/features/notifications/types/components/` + `apps/VCSM/src/features/notifications/inbox/ui/` |
| Adapters | YES | `apps/VCSM/src/features/notifications/adapters/notifications.adapter.js` |
| Lib (utility) | YES | `apps/VCSM/src/features/notifications/inbox/lib/` |
| Engine controllers | YES | `engines/notifications/src/controller/` |
| Engine DALs | YES | `engines/notifications/src/dal/` |

---

## Active Controllers

| Controller | Purpose | Auth Gate |
|---|---|---|
| `Notifications.controller.js` | Fetch inbox — calls engine `getInboxNotifications`, applies block filter, filters resolved follow requests, resolves senders, maps to domain objects | `resolveInboxActor(identity)` — gates on `identity.actorId`; returns `[]` on null |
| `NotificationsHeader.controller.js` | Returns `{ unreadCount }` via engine `countUnread`; `markAllNotificationsSeen()` uses engine `getInboxNotifications` with `autoMarkSeen: true` | Parameter guard: `if (!actorId) return { unreadCount: 0 }` |
| `notificationsCount.controller.js` | Single function `getUnreadNotificationCount` delegating to engine `countUnread` | Parameter guard: `if (!actorId) return 0` |

**Note:** `inboxUnread.controller.js` is confirmed dead (SENTRY RISK-8 — chat re-export boundary violation). Zero consumers. Pending deletion sign-off.

---

## Active DALs

| DAL | Tables | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `blocks.read.dal.js` | `moderation.blocks` | Two functions: `listBlockedActorRowsDAL` (blocker_actor_id = actor) and `listBlockingActorRowsDAL` (blocked_actor_id = actor). Explicit column selects. |
| `senders.read.dal.js` | `vc.actors`, `profiles` (public schema), vport `profiles` | Actor summary lookup via `@hydration` engine. Also provides direct `listActorIdentityRowsByIdsDAL`, `listProfileRowsByIdsDAL`, `listVportRowsByIdsDAL`. Uses `vportClient` for vport profiles. |
| `notificationRuntime.dal.js` | `notification.events`, `notification.recipients`, `notification.rendered`, `notification.inbox_items` | 301 lines — AT CONTRACT LIMIT. Cannot accept new functions; split required. Also serves as engine runtime DAL via `configureNotificationsRuntimeDAL` DI pattern. Full RPC surface: `create_event`, `insert_recipients`, `upsert_rendered`, `insert_inbox_item`, `update_recipient_status`. |

**Engine DALs (engines/notifications/src/dal/):**

| Engine DAL | Tables | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `inbox.read.dal.js` | `notification.recipients`, `notification.inbox_items` | Read-only inbox fetch and item state. Uses `notification.*` schema only. |
| Additional engine DALs | `notification.events`, `notification.rendered`, `notification.preferences`, `notification.delivery_attempts`, `notification.templates`, `notification.event_types` | Full write pipeline (events.write.dal, recipients.write.dal, rendered.write.dal, etc.) |

## Active Hooks

| Hook | Calls | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `useNotificationInbox.js` | `Notifications.controller.getNotifications`, `useSocialFollowRequestOps` (via cross-feature adapter), `useIdentity` | React Query source of truth for inbox; 60s polling; `noti:refresh` + `noti:optimistic:replace` window event listeners; badge invalidation after successful fetch |
| `useNotifications.js` | `useNotificationInbox` | Thin re-export wrapper — `useNotifications()` delegates to `useNotificationInbox()` |
| `useNotiCount.js` | `useNotificationUnread` from `@/bootstrap/bootstrap.selectors` | Thin wrapper — unread count sourced from React Query bootstrap selector |
| `useNotificationsHeader.js` | `NotificationsHeader.controller.markAllNotificationsSeen`, `useNotificationUnread` | Mark-all-seen with React Query invalidation; surfaces `{ unreadCount, markAllSeen }` |
| `useMyAppointments.js` | `useBookingOps` (booking.adapter), `dismissBooking` (@booking engine), `hydrateActorsByIds` (@hydration) | Loads actor's bookings with upcoming/pending/past segmentation; supports cancel and dismiss actions |

## Engine Dependencies

| Engine | Import Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `@notifications` | `engines/notifications/` | Core notification pipeline: `publishEvent`, `getInboxNotifications`, `countUnread`, `markSeen`, `markRead`, `dismiss`, `archive`, `configureNotificationsEngine` |
| `@hydration` | `engines/hydration/` | Actor summary hydration for sender enrichment (`hydrateAndReturnSummaries`, `hydrateActorsByIds`) |
| `@booking` | `engines/booking/` | Appointment list and dismiss operations in `useMyAppointments` hook |

---

## Cross-Feature Dependencies

| Feature | What is Imported | Direction |
|---|---|---|
| `identity` | `useIdentity` from `@/features/identity/adapters/identity.adapter` | notifications → identity (inbound: hook depends on identity context) |
| `social` | `useSocialFollowRequestOps` from `@/features/social/adapters/social.adapter` | notifications → social (inbound: follow request list for filtering resolved requests) |
| `social` | `useFollowRequestActions` from `@/features/social/adapters/friend/request/hooks/useFollowRequestActions.adapter` | notifications → social (inbound: accept/decline follow request actions from FollowRequestItem view) |
| `booking` | `useBookingOps` from `@/features/booking/adapters/booking.adapter` | notifications → booking (inbound: appointment list and cancel in useMyAppointments) |
| `shared` | `ActorLink`, `formatTimestamp` from `@/shared/` | notifications → shared (inbound: UI primitives in NotificationCard) |

All cross-feature imports from `social` and `booking` go through their declared adapter surfaces — boundary contract compliant.

---

## Authorization Pattern

The notifications feature uses an **actor-scoped parameter guard** pattern rather than an
explicit ownership assertion middleware:

1. `resolveInboxActor(identity)` — adapts the VCSM identity object (`actorId`, `kind`,
   `ownerActorId`) into inbox semantics. For `kind = 'user'`, `targetActorId = myActorId`.
   For `kind = 'vport'`, `targetActorId = vport actorId`, `myActorId = ownerActorId` (used
   for block filter). Returns `{ targetActorId: null }` on invalid identity — controller
   returns empty array and exits.
2. The engine `getInboxNotifications` and `countUnread` calls use `recipientActorId` as the
   sole filter — the caller must pass their own actor ID. No server-side ownership assertion
   at the controller level; trust is implicit via session-scoped identity.
3. Block filtering is bidirectional: blocked-by and blocking sets are both fetched and applied
   as a client-side union filter over the notification rows.
4. VPORT-specific filter: VPORTs have follow/subscribe notifications suppressed at the
   controller output level.

**ACL Gap (OPEN — IRONMAN MEDIUM):** No documented rule controls which callers may invoke
`publishVcsmNotification` / `publishVcsmNotificationBatch` via `notifications.adapter.js`.
Any authenticated feature can publish to any recipient actor ID — no ownership check at
the adapter or engine level.

---

## Module Independence Classification

**DEPENDENT**

The notifications feature depends on three external feature adapters (identity, social,
booking) and three engines (@notifications, @hydration, @booking). The engine dependency
is the architectural foundation — the feature does not own its own DB write surface
directly but rather wraps the `@notifications` engine with VCSM-specific adapter logic.
The cross-feature adapter imports (identity, social, booking) are all through declared
public adapter surfaces, making them structurally compliant but architecturally coupled.

---

## Architecture State

**EVOLVING**

The feature completed a full migration from the legacy `vc.notifications` DAL to the
`@notifications` engine (evidenced by migration comments in controllers). The runtime
DAL (`notificationRuntime.dal.js`) is a VCSM-side DI bridge that wraps engine calls —
this is the correct pattern but is at the 300-line split limit. Six dead files from the
pre-migration era remain (SENTRY REVIEW_PENDING). The core inbox and publish flows are
stable; the My Appointments panel is a structurally separate concern co-located in the
notifications screen.

---

## Known Structural Risks

1. **RISK-6 (VENOM / SENTRY — LOW):** `useMarkNotificationsRead.js` is dead code with a
   layer violation (calls `@notifications` engine directly, bypassing the controller).
   Zero consumers. Pending deletion sign-off.
2. **Dead files x5 (SENTRY — REVIEW_PENDING):** `inboxUnread.controller.js` (RISK-8 —
   chat re-export boundary violation), `useUnreadBadge.js` (RISK-7 — misattributed chat
   hook), `badgeSubscriptions.js` (noops, never called), `useNotificationsInternal.js`
   (RISK-5 — dead useState/useEffect hook), `NotiViewPostScreen.jsx` (RISK-1 — dead
   duplicate view). Six total files confirmed dead.
3. **RISK-9 (SENTRY — OPEN):** `mapSummaryRowToSender()` in `inbox/lib/resolveSenders.js`
   is a pure domain transform sitting in `lib/` instead of `model/`. Should be relocated
   to `notification.model.js`.
4. **KF-1 (KRAVEN — ELEVATED):** `publishEvent()` delivery loop in `runtime/index.js`
   is serial per-recipient — 3 awaits × N recipients = O(N × 3 × RTT). Fix:
   `Promise.allSettled` across all recipients.
5. **Publish ACL gap (IRONMAN — MEDIUM):** No enforcement layer restricts who may call
   `publishVcsmNotification()` via `notifications.adapter.js`. Any feature can publish
   to any actor ID.
6. **notificationRuntime.dal.js at 300-line limit:** Any new functions violate the VCSM
   contract limit. Must be split before adding functionality.
7. **My Appointments scope mismatch:** `screen/hooks/useMyAppointments.js` + related views
   are a booking surface co-located in the notifications screen — not a notification concern.
   No ownership documented for this placement.
8. **UI layer ownership unassigned (IRONMAN):** `inbox/ui/` (3 files) and `types/` dispatch
   layer (13 notification type views) have no documented owner.

## Recommended Handoffs

- **SPIDER-MAN** — Zero test coverage documented. No TESTS.md. Run before THOR.
- **CARNAGE** — RLS ownership for `notification.*` tables is undocumented. 5 tables
  confirmed in DB with no RLS governance: `preferences`, `delivery_attempts`, `templates`,
  `push_subscriptions`, `event_types`.
- **BLACKWIDOW** — Adversarial runtime verification never run. Required before THOR eligibility.
- **SENTRY** — Dead file deletion pending sign-off (6 files). RISK-9 transform relocation open.
- **ELEKTRA** — Precision security scan not run. Publish ACL gap needs source→sink tracing.

---

## Final Module Status

**MOSTLY COMPLETE**

Core inbox read, badge count, block filter, publish adapter, and engine integration are
all complete and structurally sound. Migration from legacy DAL to engine is done. The
feature is blocked from THOR by: dead file cleanup (6 files), publish ACL gap (MEDIUM),
KF-1 serial delivery loop (ELEVATED), no test coverage, and BLACKWIDOW never run.
